name: JV Exit Mechanism
text: Upon termination of this Agreement or upon a Party's wish to exit the Joint Venture ('Exiting Party'), the following exit mechanism shall apply: (a) the Exiting Party shall give not less than ninety (90) days' written notice of its intention to exit; (b) the remaining Party shall have the right to purchase the Exiting Party's interest at fair market value, as determined by an independent valuer mutually appointed by the Parties; (c) if the Parties fail to appoint a valuer within thirty (30) days, the valuer shall be appointed by the President of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India; and (d) upon completion of the buyout, the Exiting Party shall have no further interest in or liability to the Joint Venture. Unless the Parties execute a written transfer instrument at closing that specifies a different allocation, all assets, liabilities, and intellectual property of the Joint Venture shall be allocated in proportion to the Parties' respective contributions.

legal_basis.note: Put/call options in listed company shares require SEBI compliance

invalid_if: No exit valuation mechanism is specified
Notice period for exit is not defined
title: Exit Mechanism
dispute_hotspots: Fair market value determination — parties cannot agree on valuation methodology
Put/call options in RBI-regulated investments — FEMA compliance required
